tips: Index columns used in WHERE, JOIN, and ORDER BY clauses for faster queries
Use EXPLAIN ANALYZE to understand how your database executes queries
Normalize data to reduce redundancy, but denormalize for read performance
Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection attacks
Connection pooling reduces the overhead of establishing database connections
Use transactions for operations that must be atomic — all or nothing
Back up your database regularly and test your restore process
Use migrations to version-control your database schema changes
Avoid SELECT * in production queries — name only the columns you need
Use LIMIT and OFFSET for pagination, but consider keyset pagination for scale
Monitor slow queries with a query log or observability tool
Choose the right database type: relational, document, key-value, or graph
